beneficiary: DPIIT-recognised startups showcasing at flagship events

beneficiary: Startups per annual challenge theme (varies by chapter)

name: Prime Minister's Employment Generation Programme (PMEGP)
amount: Subsidy 15-35% of project cost (up to Rs 50 lakh for manufacturing, Rs 20 lakh for services); beneficiary contributes only 5-10%
eligibility: Individuals above 18 years, min. 8th pass for projects above Rs 10/5 lakh, no income ceiling

name: Fund of Funds for Startups (FFS) 2.0
amount: Corpus of Rs 20,000 crore (doubled from Rs 10,000 crore); invests in AIFs which invest 2x amount in startups
eligibility: Startups must be funded through SEBI-registered AIFs empanelled by SIDBI

name: Credit Linked Capital Subsidy Scheme (CLCSS)
amount: 15% upfront capital subsidy on institutional finance up to Rs 1 crore (max subsidy Rs 15 lakh)
eligibility: MSEs registered under Udyam taking term loan for approved technology upgradation in 51 sub-sectors
beneficiary: Micro and Small Enterprises upgrading technology

name: MSME Sustainable (ZED) Certification Scheme
agency: Ministry of MSME (Quality Council of India)
amount: 80% subsidy for Micro, 60% for Small, 50% for Medium enterprises on certification cost; additional 5-10% for women/SC-ST/NER units; up to Rs 5 lakh handholding support
eligibility: MSMEs registered on Udyam portal

name: Micro & Small Enterprises Cluster Development Programme (MSE-CDP)
amount: Govt grant 60-70% of project cost (Rs 5-30 crore) for Common Facility Centres; 70-80% in NE/hill/aspirational districts
eligibility: Cluster of min. 20 MSEs in geographically contiguous area
beneficiary: Clusters of micro and small manufacturing/service enterprises

name: NIDHI-EIR (Entrepreneur in Residence)
amount: Monthly stipend/fellowship support to reduce risk of taking up entrepreneurship
eligibility: Individuals mentored at NIDHI-supported incubators to become entrepreneurs

name: NSIC Raw Material Assistance Scheme
amount: Financing for procurement of raw material (indigenous & imported) up to 180 days
eligibility: Registered MSMEs with valid Udyam registration
beneficiary: Micro and small enterprises needing raw material funding

name: NSIC Single Point Registration Scheme (SPRS)
amount: Eligibility for benefits under Public Procurement Policy for Micro & Small Enterprises (25% procurement reservation)
eligibility: MSEs with Udyam registration and manufacturing/service facility
beneficiary: Micro and small enterprises seeking govt procurement access

name: Next Generation Incubation Scheme (NGIS)
agency: Software Technology Parks of India (STPI) / MeitY
amount: Seed fund up to Rs 25 lakh; pre-incubation stipend Rs 10,000/month for 6 months; total outlay Rs 95.03 crore for 300 startups
eligibility: Startups in 12 Tier-II locations (Agartala, Bhopal, Guwahati, Jaipur, Lucknow, etc.)
beneficiary: Software product startups in Tier-II cities

name: Khadi and Village Industries Commission (KVIC) Interest Subsidy Eligibility Certificate Scheme
amount: Low interest rate of 4% via interest subsidy eligibility certificate
eligibility: Khadi/village industry units availing bank loans under KVIC schemes

name: Raising and Accelerating MSME Performance (RAMP)
amount: Total outlay Rs 6,062.45 crore (USD 808 million); targets 5.5 lakh MSMEs over 5 years
eligibility: MSMEs benefiting through enhanced implementation of existing MoMSME schemes

name: Trade Receivables Discounting System (TReDS)
amount: Enables MSMEs to discount invoices and receive payment within 24 hours of bid acceptance
eligibility: MSME sellers with receivables from corporate/PSU/govt buyers registered on TReDS
beneficiary: MSME sellers with receivables from large buyers

name: Mahila e-Haat
agency: Ministry of Women and Child Development
amount: Free online platform to showcase/sell products with no listing or commission fees
eligibility: Women entrepreneurs, SHGs and NGOs led by women across sectors

name: Scheme of Fund for Regeneration of Traditional Industries (SFURTI)
agency: Ministry of MSME (KVIC and Coir Board)
amount: Financial assistance up to 90% (95% in NER/J&K/Hill areas) of hard intervention cost; full soft intervention cost covered
eligibility: Traditional industry/artisan clusters organized for cluster development

name: A Scheme for Promotion of Innovation, Rural Industries and Entrepreneurship (ASPIRE)
amount: Up to Rs 1 crore to govt agencies / Rs 75 lakh to private agencies for plant & machinery; up to Rs 1 crore for operational expenditure
eligibility: Institutions/agencies setting up Livelihood Business Incubators in rural/agro sectors
beneficiary: Rural and agro-based entrepreneurs, Livelihood Business Incubators

name: National SC-ST Hub Scheme (NSSH)
amount: Support for plant & machinery, capacity building, market linkage, exhibition participation, Udyam/GeM onboarding
eligibility: SC/ST owned micro and small enterprises

name: Agriculture Infrastructure Fund (AIF)
agency: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
amount: 3% per annum interest subvention on loans up to Rs 2 crore for 7 years; credit guarantee coverage under CGTMSE/CGFMU
eligibility: Farmers, FPOs, PACS, entrepreneurs, startups engaged in agri-infrastructure/post-harvest projects

name: Coir Udyami Yojana
amount: 40% subsidy on project cost up to Rs 10 lakh; beneficiary contributes 5%, bank credit 55%
eligibility: Individuals, SHGs, NGOs, cooperatives, companies setting up new coir units

name: Amended Technology Upgradation Fund Scheme (ATUFS)
amount: 15% subsidy (cap Rs 30 crore) for garments/technical textiles; 10% subsidy (cap Rs 20 crore) for other textile subsectors
eligibility: Companies/LLPs/MSMEs in textile sector taking term loan for technology upgradation
beneficiary: Textile sector MSMEs and companies upgrading technology

name: Market Development Assistance (MDA) Scheme
amount: 75% airfare reimbursement (economy class) and 50% space rental for general category; 100% for women/SC-ST/NER entrepreneurs
eligibility: MSEs with permanent registration with Directorate of Industries/DIC, 3 years audited accounts
beneficiary: MSME exporters participating in international trade fairs

name: Venture Capital Assistance Scheme (SFAC)
amount: Interest-free loan as equity component, repayable after term loan repayment
eligibility: Individuals, FPOs, agripreneurs, agri-graduates setting up agri-business projects

name: Promoting Innovations in Individuals, Start-ups and MSMEs (PRISM)
agency: Department of Scientific and Industrial Research (DSIR)
amount: Phase I: PoC/prototype support; Phase II: up to Rs 50 lakh for commercialization
eligibility: Individual Indian innovators with original technological ideas

name: Credit Card Scheme for Artisans and Weavers
agency: Ministry of Textiles / Development Commissioner Handicrafts
amount: Credit limit up to Rs 2 lakh; 7% interest subvention for 5 years; no collateral required
eligibility: Artisans and weavers selected by District-level Committee

name: Credit Guarantee Scheme for Startups (CGSS)
amount: Guarantee cover up to Rs 20 crore per borrower (85% cover up to Rs 10 crore, 75% cover Rs 10-20 crore)
eligibility: DPIIT-recognized startup not in default, company age not exceeding 10 years

name: Startup Tax Exemption under Section 80-IAC
amount: 100% tax exemption on profits for 3 consecutive years out of first 10 years since incorporation
eligibility: DPIIT-recognized startup incorporated after 1 April 2016, turnover below Rs 100 crore, less than 10 years old

name: Angel Tax Exemption under Section 56(2)(viib)
amount: Exemption from tax on share premium received above fair market value
eligibility: DPIIT-recognized startup that has filed declaration (Form 2) with DPIIT

name: Bharat Startup Grand Challenge
amount: Platform connecting startups with corporate/govt problem statements; no direct funding disclosed, free to apply
eligibility: DPIIT-recognized or eligible startups addressing published challenge statements
beneficiary: Startups solving real-world industry and societal problems

name: Trade Related Entrepreneurship Assistance and Development (TREAD)
amount: Grant up to 30% of project cost for women entrepreneurs; balance financed through loan from lending institutions
eligibility: Women entrepreneurs setting up non-farm sector enterprises, associations of women

name: NEDFi Opportunity Scheme for Small Enterprises (NoSSE)
amount: Loan up to Rs 1 crore for projects between Rs 50 lakh and Rs 200 lakh, interest around 8% p.a.
eligibility: First-generation entrepreneurs setting up new small enterprises in NE states
beneficiary: First-generation entrepreneurs in North East India

name: NEDFi Rupee Term Loan (RTL) Scheme
amount: Term loan financing exceeding Rs 10 crore per project for manufacturing/service sector/agri-allied projects
eligibility: Enterprises setting up projects in the North Eastern states
beneficiary: MSMEs and large enterprises in North East India

name: ELEVATE / Idea2PoC Grant-in-Aid Scheme
agency: Department of Electronics, IT, BT & S&T, Govt of Karnataka (Startup Karnataka / KDEM)
amount: One-time grant-in-aid up to Rs 50 lakh, disbursed in milestone-based installments, no equity taken
eligibility: Startups registered in Karnataka with innovative product/technology idea
beneficiary: Early-stage innovative tech startups, women entrepreneurs

name: KSUM Seed Support Scheme
amount: Seed support up to Rs 15 lakh as soft loan at 6% interest, 12-month moratorium, 36-month repayment
eligibility: Startup registered in Kerala as LLP/Pvt Ltd with active KSUM Unique ID, CIBIL score preferably 750+

name: KSUM Idea Grant
amount: Grant support for idea-stage validation and prototype development
eligibility: Early-stage innovators/students with KSUM registration

name: Telangana Patent Reimbursement Scheme
amount: Rs 2 lakh reimbursement for domestic patents; Rs 10 lakh for international patents
eligibility: Startups registered with TSIC filing for patent protection

name: GUSEC Seed (NIDHI-SSS)
agency: Gujarat University Startup and Entrepreneurship Council (GUSEC)
amount: Seed funding support up to Rs 50 lakh via equity/debt/mix, under NIDHI-SSS
eligibility: Startups incubated at GUSEC working in deep-tech domains
beneficiary: Deep-tech startups (AI, SpaceTech, IoT, ML, defence-tech)

name: Gujarat Startup Scheme (Innovators Scheme)
amount: Up to Rs 30 lakh grant for prototype/testing/marketing; monthly allowance Rs 20,000 (Rs 25,000 with woman co-founder) for 12 months
eligibility: Startups/innovators registered under Gujarat Startup Policy

name: iStart Rajasthan Pre-Seed Grant
agency: Department of IT&C, Govt of Rajasthan (iStart)
amount: Grant of Rs 2,40,000; additional Rs 60,000 if women hold over 50% equity
eligibility: Pre-seed stage startup registered under iStart program meeting evaluation criteria

name: iStart Rajasthan Seed Funding
agency: Department of IT&C, Govt of Rajasthan (iStart)
amount: Seed funding up to Rs 10 lakh plus monthly allowance Rs 10,000-25,000 depending on stage/location
eligibility: Startups registered on iStart Rajasthan platform

name: iStart Rajasthan Scale-Up Fund
agency: Department of IT&C, Govt of Rajasthan (iStart)
amount: Financial support up to 50% of total amount raised, subject to ceilings
eligibility: Growth-stage startups registered under iStart Rajasthan

name: MSInS Seed Fund Scheme
amount: Funding up to Rs 10 lakh for product development, market validation, business development
eligibility: Startups registered with MSInS/Startup India meeting eligibility criteria

name: Chief Minister MahaFund
amount: Rs 500 crore fund; loans between Rs 5-10 lakh for eligible founders
eligibility: Founders meeting MahaFund eligibility criteria under Maharashtra Startup Policy 2025
beneficiary: Startups, especially youth, women, rural entrepreneurs

name: UP Startup Policy - Prototype Development Grant
agency: StartinUP, Dept of IT & Electronics, Govt of UP
amount: Prototype grant up to Rs 5 lakh for MVP development
eligibility: Startups recognized under UP Startup Policy 2020

name: UP Startup Policy - Seed Capital/Marketing Assistance
agency: StartinUP, Dept of IT & Electronics, Govt of UP
amount: Seed capital/marketing assistance up to Rs 7.5 lakh, disbursed in 40-30-30% milestone tranches
eligibility: Startups with MVP ready seeking market launch support, registered with StartinUP

name: UP Startup Policy - Sustenance Allowance
agency: StartinUP, Dept of IT & Electronics, Govt of UP
amount: Rs 17,500 per month per startup for 1 year (up to 25 startups per incubator per year)
eligibility: Idea-stage startups at recognized incubators in UP; 50% extra for women/transgender/Divyangjan co-founders

name: Punjab Startup Seed Grant
amount: Seed grant up to Rs 3 lakh per startup for concept validation, prototyping, market research, marketing
eligibility: Startups registered under Startup Punjab initiative

name: Punjab Innovation Fund
amount: Rs 150 crore fund to invest in early-stage startups
eligibility: Early-stage startups in Punjab meeting fund investment criteria

name: Punjab Interest Subsidy for Startups
amount: 8% annual interest subsidy on loans, up to Rs 5 lakh per year for 5 years
eligibility: Startups registered under Startup Punjab with active bank/NBFC loans

name: MP Startup Policy Seed Capital Fund
agency: MP State Employment Generation and Development Corporation (MPSEDC)
amount: Rs 100 crore Startup Capital Fund invested via empanelled AIFs; Rs 30 lakh seed grant via empanelled incubators
eligibility: DPIIT-recognized startups in Madhya Pradesh routed through state-recognized incubators

name: MP Investment Assistance Grant
agency: MPSEDC / Dept of Skill Development, Govt of MP
amount: 15% grant assistance on funds raised (cap Rs 15 lakh, up to 4 times); 18% (cap Rs 18 lakh) for women/SC/ST entrepreneurs
eligibility: Startups raising funds from SEBI/RBI recognized institutions, DPIIT-recognized

name: AP Innovation & Startup Policy - Scaling Grant
amount: Scaling grant up to Rs 15 lakh for MVP development, testing, commercialization
eligibility: Startups registered under AP Innovation & Startup Policy 4.0

name: AP Inclusive Startup Grant
amount: Phased support up to Rs 20 lakh total per startup
eligibility: Startups led by women/SC/ST/BC/minority entrepreneurs registered under AP policy
beneficiary: Women, SC/ST, BC and minority entrepreneurs

name: Delhi Startup Grant (existing scheme, pending policy)
agency: Dept of Industries, Govt of NCT of Delhi
amount: Equity-free grant of up to Rs 10 lakh to selected startups
eligibility: DPIIT-recognized startups registered in Delhi

name: West Bengal Startup Seed Fund
amount: Grants/seed funding ranging from Rs 5-25 lakh; monthly sustenance allowance Rs 10,000-25,000
eligibility: Startups registered with Startup Bengal in priority sectors (IT, agro-processing, textiles, etc.)

name: Startup Odisha Monthly Allowance / Incentives
amount: Monthly allowance Rs 20,000 (Rs 22,000 for women/transgender/SC-ST-SEBC/PH founders) for 1 year; 50% incubation rent subsidy up to Rs 5,000/month
eligibility: Recognized startup entities registered with Startup Odisha

name: Haryana Seed Funding Scheme for Startups
agency: Dept of Industries and Commerce, Govt of Haryana (Startup Haryana)
amount: Seed grant up to Rs 10 lakh per startup for idea validation, PoC, prototype development
eligibility: DPIIT-recognized startup registered with Startup Haryana, office located in Haryana

name: Bihar Startup Matching Grant
agency: Industries Department, Govt of Bihar (Startup Bihar)
amount: Matching grant up to Rs 3 lakh (or specified proportion) equal to investor funding raised
eligibility: Startups under Bihar Startup Policy that have raised angel/VC investment

name: Assam Startup Revenue Grant
amount: Funding up to Rs 5 lakh per startup for revenue-generating innovative product/service
eligibility: Startups registered under Startup Assam with innovative revenue-generating idea

name: Assam Startup Venture Capital Fund
agency: SIDBI Venture Capital / Govt of Assam
amount: SEBI-registered Category I AIF Venture Capital Fund investing in scalable Assam-based ventures
eligibility: Early and growth-stage startups/MSMEs based in Assam

name: HIM Startup Yojana Seed Fund
amount: Seed funding up to Rs 25 lakh plus rental reimbursement and tax benefits
eligibility: Startups incubated in HP-approved incubators, DPIIT-recognized, operating in Himachal Pradesh
beneficiary: Early-stage startups and innovators in HP

name: Jharkhand Startup Seed Grant
agency: Dept of IT-e-Governance, Govt of Jharkhand
amount: Seed funding up to Rs 25-50 lakh through government-approved incubators
eligibility: Startups registered under Jharkhand Startup Policy 2023 via approved incubators

name: Jharkhand Startup Marketing Grant
agency: Dept of IT-e-Governance, Govt of Jharkhand
amount: One-time marketing grant up to Rs 10 lakh; monthly stipend Rs 5,000 per founder (up to 3) for 1 year
eligibility: Startups registered under Jharkhand Startup Policy

name: Chhattisgarh Startup Seed Funding
agency: Dept of Commerce & Industries, Govt of Chhattisgarh
amount: Seed funding up to Rs 10 lakh for MVP development
eligibility: Startups registered under Chhattisgarh Innovation and Startup Promotion Policy 2025-30

name: Chhattisgarh Startup Capital Fund
agency: Dept of Commerce & Industries, Govt of Chhattisgarh
amount: Rs 100 crore Startup Capital Fund invested via SEBI-registered AIFs
eligibility: Startups registered under Chhattisgarh Startup Policy 2025-30

name: Chhattisgarh Credit Risk Fund / Interest Subsidy
agency: Dept of Commerce & Industries, Govt of Chhattisgarh
amount: Rs 50 crore Credit Risk Fund for collateral-free loans up to Rs 1 crore; interest subsidy up to 75% (cap Rs 50 lakh) for 5 years
eligibility: Startups registered under Chhattisgarh Innovation and Startup Promotion Policy

name: Goa Startup Seed Capital Scheme
agency: Start-up Information Technology Promotion Cell (SITPC), Govt of Goa
amount: One-time grant of Rs 10 lakh for startups with MVP ready to scale
eligibility: Startups registered under Startup Goa Policy with a developed MVP

name: Uttarakhand Startup Seed Funding Grant
amount: Seed funding up to Rs 15 lakh plus monthly allowance up to Rs 22,000
eligibility: Startups registered under Uttarakhand Startup Policy 2023

name: Devbhoomi Udyamita Yojana - Student Startup Seed Fund Award
agency: Dept of Higher Education, Govt of Uttarakhand
amount: Financial assistance of Rs 75,000 per selected business idea
eligibility: Students in Uttarakhand higher education institutions with viable business idea

name: Uttarakhand Mahila Udyami Vishesh Protsahan Yojana
agency: Directorate of Industries, Govt of Uttarakhand
amount: Up to 25% capital subsidy plus 6% interest subsidy
eligibility: Women entrepreneurs setting up enterprises in Uttarakhand

name: J&K Startup Policy Seed Fund
agency: JKEDI / Industries and Commerce Dept, J&K
amount: Seed funding up to Rs 20 lakh in four equal installments for prototype development, market entry, scaling
eligibility: Startups recognized under J&K Startup Policy 2024-27

name: NEDFi Venture Capital / Startup Support (North East)
amount: Equity/venture capital assistance to scalable startups and MSMEs in NE states
eligibility: Startups/MSMEs incorporated and operating in North Eastern states
beneficiary: Startups and small enterprises in North Eastern states

name: Meghalaya Startup Policy (under revamp)
amount: New policy replacing earlier grant-based model; focus on market access and value chain integration (amounts under revision)
eligibility: Startups/entrepreneurs registered under Meghalaya Startup Policy

name: Arunachal Pradesh Innovation and Investment Park (APIIP) Support
agency: Dept of Industries, Govt of Arunachal Pradesh
amount: Access to Startup India Kit and state innovation park infrastructure and support services
eligibility: Startups/entrepreneurs registered under Arunachal Pradesh startup initiatives
beneficiary: Startups and innovators in Arunachal Pradesh

name: SIDBI Make in India Soft Loan Fund for MSME (SMILE)
amount: Soft loan up to Rs 20 lakh (10% of project cost) general; up to Rs 30 lakh (15%) for SC/ST/Women/PwD-owned units; repayment up to 10 years
eligibility: MSMEs under Make in India identified sectors meeting SIDBI eligibility norms
beneficiary: New and existing MSMEs in 25 Make in India sectors

name: Karnataka Industrial Policy 2025-30 - MSME Capital Investment Subsidy
agency: Department of Industries and Commerce, Govt of Karnataka
amount: Up to 35% of eligible fixed capital investment (zone-dependent); capital subsidy up to Rs 1 crore for small/medium enterprises
eligibility: MSMEs setting up new units or expansion under Karnataka Industrial Policy 2025-30
beneficiary: Micro, small and medium manufacturing enterprises

name: Karnataka MSME Interest Subsidy
agency: Department of Industries and Commerce, Govt of Karnataka
amount: Interest subsidy up to 6% on term loans, max loan amount Rs 50 lakh
eligibility: MSMEs availing term loans under Karnataka Industrial Policy

name: Tamil Nadu New Entrepreneur-cum-Enterprise Development Scheme (NEEDS)
agency: MSME Department, Govt of Tamil Nadu / TIIC
amount: 25% capital subsidy (cap Rs 75 lakh); 3% interest subvention throughout loan repayment; project cost Rs 10 lakh-5 crore
eligibility: First-generation entrepreneurs with degree/diploma/ITI, manufacturing or service project
beneficiary: First-generation entrepreneurs aged 21-45 (up to 55 for special categories)

name: Tamil Nadu MSME Capital Subsidy Scheme
agency: MSME Department, Govt of Tamil Nadu
amount: 25% capital subsidy on eligible plant & machinery value, subject to max Rs 150 lakh
eligibility: MSMEs registered under Udyam investing in new plant and machinery in Tamil Nadu
beneficiary: Micro, small and medium manufacturing enterprises

name: Aatmanirbhar Gujarat Scheme for MSMEs - Capital Investment Subsidy
agency: Industries and Mines Department, Govt of Gujarat
amount: Up to 25% of eligible fixed capital investment, max Rs 25 lakh
eligibility: Micro enterprises setting up new units/expansion under Aatmanirbhar Gujarat Scheme 2022-27

name: Aatmanirbhar Gujarat Scheme - Interest Subsidy for MSMEs
agency: Industries and Mines Department, Govt of Gujarat
amount: Interest subsidy on term loans, varying by taluka classification
eligibility: MSMEs availing term loans for new/expansion units under Aatmanirbhar Gujarat Scheme

name: Maharashtra Package Scheme of Incentives (PSI) 2019 - Industrial Promotion Subsidy
agency: Directorate of Industries, Govt of Maharashtra
amount: 100% SGST refund on first sale in Maharashtra; interest subsidy 5% p.a. on term loans (B/C/D/D+ regions)
eligibility: New or expanding MSME manufacturing units registered under PSI-2019 in eligible zones
beneficiary: MSMEs setting up new/expansion manufacturing units

name: Punjab Industrial Policy 2022 - Capital Subsidy for MSMEs
agency: Department of Industries and Commerce, Govt of Punjab
amount: 30% of Fixed Capital Investment, up to max Rs 30 lakh per unit
eligibility: New MSME units or units undertaking expansion (min. 25% of original investment) in Punjab
beneficiary: Micro, small and medium manufacturing enterprises

name: Rajasthan Investment Promotion Scheme (RIPS) 2024
agency: Bureau of Investment Promotion, Govt of Rajasthan
amount: Up to 75% SGST reimbursement for 10 years; 50% EPF/ESI refund for 7 years; interest subvention and capital subsidies
eligibility: Eligible MSME projects approved on or before 31 March 2029 under RIPS 2024

name: Uttar Pradesh MSME Promotion Policy 2022 - Capital Subsidy
agency: Department of MSME, Govt of Uttar Pradesh
amount: Capital subsidy 10-25% depending on region; additional 2% for SC/ST and women entrepreneurs; disbursed in 2 installments
eligibility: MSMEs setting up units in UP under MSME Promotion Policy 2022
beneficiary: Micro, small and medium enterprises in Bundelkhand/Purvanchal/Madhyanchal/Paschimanchal

name: Uttar Pradesh MSME Interest Subsidy Scheme
agency: Department of MSME, Govt of Uttar Pradesh
amount: 50% interest subsidy payable annually for 5 years, capped at Rs 25 lakh per unit; 7-year infrastructure interest subsidy
eligibility: Micro units availing term loans under UP MSME Promotion Policy 2022

name: Haryana Gramin Udyogik Vikas Yojana
agency: Directorate of MSME, Govt of Haryana
amount: 15% capital subsidy (max Rs 15 lakh; up to Rs 20 lakh for women/SC/ST) on plant, machinery and building investment
eligibility: Micro enterprises setting up outside municipal limits in rural Haryana blocks
beneficiary: Micro enterprises in rural areas (B/C/D category blocks)

name: Haryana MSME Interest Subsidy Scheme
agency: Directorate of MSME, Govt of Haryana
amount: 6% interest subsidy for micro enterprises (max Rs 8 lakh/year for 5 years); 5-8% for other segments over 5-7 years
eligibility: New MSMEs/startups/import substitution enterprises under Haryana Enterprises & Employment Policy 2020
beneficiary: Micro, small and medium enterprises, startups

name: Kerala Entrepreneur Support Scheme (ESS)
agency: Directorate of Industries and Commerce, Govt of Kerala
amount: 15% subsidy (cap Rs 30 lakh) general category; 25% (cap Rs 40 lakh) for youth/women/SC-ST/NRK; additional 10% for priority sectors/backward districts
eligibility: New MSMEs registered under Udyam in Kerala; loan from financial institution not mandatory
beneficiary: Micro, small and medium manufacturing/service enterprises

name: Odisha MSME Capital Investment Subsidy
agency: Industrial Promotion and Investment Corporation of Odisha (IPICOL)
amount: 25% subsidy on machinery investment (max Rs 2 crore); 30% (max Rs 2.5 crore) for SC/ST/women/technically qualified entrepreneurs
eligibility: MSMEs setting up new manufacturing units under Odisha IPR 2022
beneficiary: Micro, small and medium manufacturing enterprises

name: Odisha MSME Interest Subsidy
agency: Industrial Promotion and Investment Corporation of Odisha (IPICOL)
amount: 50% interest subsidy on interest paid on bank term loans
eligibility: MSME units availing bank term loans under Odisha MSME Development Policy 2022
beneficiary: Micro, small and medium enterprises with bank term loans

name: Interest Equalisation Scheme on Pre and Post Shipment Rupee Export Credit
amount: 3% interest rate reduction on pre-shipment and post-shipment rupee export credit; capped at Rs 50 lakh benefit per exporter per fiscal year
eligibility: MSME manufacturer exporters availing rupee export credit from eligible banks

name: Public Procurement Policy for Micro and Small Enterprises (MSE) Order 2012
amount: Mandatory 25% annual procurement from MSEs by central ministries/CPSEs (incl. 4% from SC/ST-owned, 3% from women-owned MSEs); 15% price preference
eligibility: MSEs registered under Udyam and enlisted with NSIC/relevant procuring agency
beneficiary: Micro and Small Enterprises supplying to government

name: West Bengal Banglashree Scheme for MSMEs
agency: MSME & Textiles Department, Govt of West Bengal
amount: 10-30% capital subsidy; interest subsidy up to 6% for 5 years; plus power subsidy, stamp duty and SGST reimbursement
eligibility: MSMEs with valid Udyam registration setting up/expanding manufacturing units in West Bengal

name: Delhi MSME Financial Assistance Scheme (Secondary/Tertiary/Services Sector)
agency: Department of Industries, Govt of NCT of Delhi
amount: Financial assistance up to Rs 2 lakh to individual entrepreneurs/artisans; subsidy of 15% of project cost up to Rs 7,500
eligibility: Individual entrepreneurs, artisans and trained professionals in secondary/tertiary/services sectors in Delhi

name: Yuva Sahakar - Cooperative Enterprise Support and Innovation Scheme
agency: National Cooperative Development Corporation (NCDC), Ministry of Cooperation
amount: 2% interest subvention on term loan (project cost up to Rs 3 crore); funding up to 70-80% of project cost; 2-year moratorium; Rs 1000 crore CSIF corpus
eligibility: New cooperative societies in operation for minimum 3 months with young entrepreneurs/innovative ideas

name: PM Vishwakarma Scheme
amount: Collateral-free loans up to Rs 3 lakh in 2 tranches at 5% interest (8% govt subvention); Rs 15,000 toolkit incentive; Rs 500/day training stipend
eligibility: Artisans/craftspeople engaged in one of 18 identified traditional trades, verified through skill training
beneficiary: Artisans and craftspeople in 18 traditional trades
